Track 3 · Unicorn

Data Wizard

The data team for companies that don't have one.

Upload a file, build a governed lakehouse, ask questions in plain English and publish a Tableau dashboard — by typing a sentence into Slack. Every act of reasoning runs on Gemma, open-source, on an AMD GPU.

AMD Instinct GPU AMD Developer Cloud ROCm Gemma 4 · 31B Gemma Vision Fireworks AI
Slack · Bolt + Block Kit Databricks · Unity Catalog Tableau REST
01
The Problem

The people who own the data
are not the people who can query it.

  • ✦The office manager holds the signup sheet. The clinic administrator holds a folder of scanned invoices. The sales lead holds the spreadsheet.
  • ✦None of them write SQL. And there is no engineer down the hall to ask.
  • ✦So the question takes three days — or, far more often, never gets asked at all, and the decision gets made on instinct.
  • ✦Meanwhile a scanned invoice gets retyped by hand, one row at a time.
  • ✦The tooling that fixes this — a lakehouse, a medallion pipeline, a BI server — is real, mature, and completely out of reach, because every inch of it assumes a technical operator.
33M+
Small businesses in the US alone
(SBA)
0
Data engineers most of them employ
Data Wizard removes the operator. The interface is a sentence in Slack. The operator is Gemma.
02
Why AI is Essential

This problem is unsolvable without a model.

Every core capability requires understanding of language or of images. None of it can be done with rules, regexes or templates — we tried the deterministic version first, and it is exactly why this category has never reached non-technical users.

Language → SQL, against a live schema

"How many signups per country?" has no fixed answer. The model must read the actual tables and columns that exist right now, resolve which one the user means, and emit one correct Databricks statement. A template can't do it because the schema is different for every customer.

Language → DDL

"Create a schema called sales" is object creation in natural language. Creating things — not just reading — is where non-technical users are normally forced back to a console.

Vision: paper → table

A scanned invoice is a picture. There are no cells, no delimiters, no encoding — just pixels. Extracting a typed table means understanding layout, alignment and meaning. OCR returns characters; we need structure.

Vision: whiteboard → table In progress

A table drawn by hand, photographed at an angle, in bad light. No rule-based system reads that. A vision model does. Built, not yet verified — the same Gemma vision path as the scanned page.

Choosing the right chart

"Create a dashboard" doesn't say bar, line or map — nor which column is the dimension and which the measure. The model has to look at the data's shape and decide, the way an analyst would.

Judging intent before destroying data

Distinguishing "show me inactive users" from "delete the inactive users" is a semantic judgement, and getting it wrong is unrecoverable.

03
Distribution

Why Slack?

The hardest problem in selling data tooling to non-technical people is not the tooling. It is that they will not log in to it.

Zero new software, zero training

A BI tool asks a busy administrator to learn a console, remember a password, and change their day. Slack asks them to type a sentence in the window they already have open. Adoption is the product's real bottleneck — so we removed it entirely.

The data conversation already happens here

"Hey, how many signups did we get?" is already a Slack message today — it just gets sent to a colleague who then does it by hand. We answer the message that is already being sent.

The answer lands where the decision is made

A dashboard nobody opens changes nothing. A table posted into the channel where the team is arguing changes the argument.

It is also the audit trail

Every question, every generated statement, and every confirmation is a message with a name and a timestamp, in a channel. Governance that nobody had to configure.

Multiplayer by default

One person uploads the CSV; the whole team can query it. The knowledge doesn't live on someone's laptop.

Built on the real surface

Bolt + Socket Mode, and the new Block Kit agent components — Data Table for results, Card for actions, modals for destinations. Not a text bot pretending to be an app.

And it's personal — I chose Slack from experience. I'm a data engineer, and even for me it's bothersome to connect to one more platform, wait for a console, click past gadgets I never asked for. I just want to ask the question and send the answer to the person who needs it — a coworker, an internal user, a customer. Slack gives exactly that: one simple surface, nothing extra. If it's the path of least resistance for an engineer, imagine what it is for the office manager.
04
The Sharpest Case

Healthcare still runs on paper.

Scanned documents are not a legacy edge case. They are how much of the economy still operates — and nowhere more than in health.

Walk into a hospital, a clinic, a medical billing office

You will find scanned intake forms. Faxed lab results. Photographed insurance cards. Invoices that are pictures of paper, not data. This is not Tuesday's exception. This is Tuesday.

Today, a human retypes it — one row at a time

It is slow. It is expensive. And on clinical and billing data, every retyped row is a transcription-error surface on numbers that actually matter.

The person doing it is an administrator, not an engineer

They have no data team to escalate to. They are, precisely and exactly, the user this product was built for.

A scanned invoice — a picture, not data. Gemma's vision on the AMD GPU reads the table off the page image.

Verified end-to-end

Rasterise the page → hand the image to Gemma's vision on the AMD GPU → a typed, queryable table in the lakehouse. Tested on a real scanned invoice, extracted 100% accurately.

05
Why AMD

A scanned patient record is data you are
not allowed to send to a closed API.

This is the slide that matters. For most projects, the GPU is infrastructure. For this one, it is the reason the product is legal.

The constraint is regulatory, not technical

Protected health information is bound by HIPAA in the US and GDPR in the EU. For a hospital, sending that page to a third-party model endpoint is not a trade-off to weigh. It is not permitted.

An open model on your own AMD GPU is the answer

The page never leaves the building. No third-party processor. No data-sharing agreement to negotiate. No vendor retention policy to audit. The same is true of the ledger, the payroll file and the customer list.

So AMD is not an implementation detail

A closed-API version of Data Wizard could not be deployed in a hospital at all. Take the AMD GPU away and you do not get a slower product — in regulated industries, you get no product.

The data producer loads the lake directly

Every intermediary is a person reading records they don't need to see — a spreadsheet on a laptop, a file emailed to a colleague, a copy nobody tracks. With Data Wizard, the person who produces the data sends it straight into the governed lakehouse — no analyst, no engineer, no hand-offs. The fewer people involved, the smaller the leak surface — for the most sensitive records, the shortest chain is the safest one: read by one model, on your own GPU, and by nobody else.

This is the whole commercial thesis. The customers who need Data Wizard most — clinics, practices, billing offices, family firms — are exactly the customers who can least afford to hand their data to somebody else. Open weights on hardware they control is not a preference. It is the only adoptable architecture.
Scope note: self-hosting is what makes compliance possible. It is not itself a certification — Data Wizard is a prototype, not a HIPAA-attested system.
06
Our Solution

Everything below happens in a Slack message.

No console. No SQL. No BI licence. No onboarding.

Ingest anything

Drop a CSV and every column type is inferred. Drop a scanned PDF and Gemma's vision reads the table off the image. Draw a table on the whiteboard In progress

Ask in plain English

"How many signups per country?" · "Top 3 by score" · "Which countries in the GDP table are in Latin America?" Gemma reads the live schema and writes the SQL.

Create in plain English

"Create a schema called sales." "What catalogs are there?" Gemma writes the DDL too — object creation is natural language, not a console.

A governed lakehouse, unknowingly

"Build a medallion pipeline" → bronze / silver / gold, with lineage, dedup and an aggregation dimension chosen from the data. They never learn the words.

Dashboards from a sentence

"Create a dashboard with signups" → Gemma picks the chart, the dimension and the measure; a real Tableau workbook is published and the chart posts back into the channel.

07
Use of AMD Platform

Gemma isn't a feature of this product.
Gemma is the product's reasoning.

gemma4:31b served with Ollama on an AMD Instinct GPU, on AMD Developer Cloud, under ROCm — driving four independent capabilities. There is no fallback path in which a human writes the SQL.

JOB 01

Natural language → SQL

Reads the live schema, writes one Databricks statement, and explains it in a sentence.

AMD GPU
JOB 02

Natural language → DDL

CREATE SCHEMA, CREATE TABLE, SHOW, DESCRIBE — all model-authored, then safety-classified.

AMD GPU
JOB 03

Vision → structured table

Scanned invoices and intake forms become typed rows. Hand-drawn whiteboards use the same path In progress

AMD GPUFireworks fallback
JOB 04

Chart selection

Chooses chart type, dimension, measure and aggregation from one sentence — then it's published to Tableau.

AMD GPU
Fireworks AI serves the same open Gemma family as an automatic fallback on the vision path, so a GPU cold-start never becomes a failed user request. Open weights, end to end — no closed model anywhere in the reasoning loop.
08
How It Works

The whole journey is four messages.

STEP 01

Drop the file

CSV, scanned PDF, or a whiteboard photo. Gemma types every column — and reads the image if it needs to.

STEP 02

Name it in your words

We never name a table after the file. Real exports are called q3_export_final_v2. You get asked what you call it — with a cleaned suggestion to accept.

STEP 03

Choose its home

A new table, or an existing one — append or replace. Mismatched columns are refused, not silently written into the wrong place.

STEP 04

Ask anything

Questions, pipelines, dashboards. In English. Forever.

Bronze — raw rows exactly as given, plus lineage
Silver — deduplicated, empties dropped, types enforced
Gold — aggregated on a dimension that actually groups
The gold dimension is chosen by measuring cardinality — grouping 10 signups by full_name gives 10 groups of 1, which is useless. It picks country_code.
09
System Architecture

Data Wizard — Slack in, governed lakehouse out

Reasoning and vision both execute on one AMD GPU through ROCm. Every statement Gemma writes is classified before it runs.

10
Tech Stack

Everything in the box, layer by layer

Six systems, one conversation. Open-weight reasoning on AMD in the middle — everything else is plumbing that lets a Slack message reach a governed lakehouse and come back as an answer.

💬 Slack

Bolt for JavaScript in Socket Mode. Results render as real Block Kit surfaces — Data Table for answers, Cards for actions and confirmations.

Bolt · Socket ModeBlock KitCard + Data Table

🧠 AI / LLM

Gemma 4 · 31B served by Ollama on an AMD Instinct MI300X via ROCm. Fireworks AI hosts the same open family as vision fallback; OpenAI generates synthetic rows, Perplexity grounds real ones with citations.

AMD Instinct · ROCmOllama · gemma4:31bFireworksOpenAIPerplexity

🗄 Data

Databricks — Unity Catalog + a serverless SQL warehouse over the Statement Execution API. Medallion bronze / silver / gold, an SQL safety classifier, and a custom RFC-4180 CSV parser.

DatabricksUnity CatalogMedallionSQL guard

📊 Visualization

Gemma picks the chart; we generate a .twb, package a .twbx, publish through the Tableau REST API, and render the PNG back into the channel. Extensions API for voice-filtered dashboards.

Tableau REST.twb → .twbx → PNGExtensions API

🎙 Voice

An ElevenLabs Conversational AI agent calls the same NL→SQL path through a query_data webhook, speaks the answer, and posts every transcript back into Slack.

ElevenLabsWebhook toolSlack transcripts

🔌 Infra & protocol

Node.js ESM + Express across every service. An MCP server (Streamable HTTP) exposes the same tools to Claude, Cursor, and ChatGPT; cloudflared / ngrok tunnels handle the HTTPS edge.

Node.js · ExpressMCP servercloudflared / ngrok
11
Safety

A model that can write CREATE TABLE
can also write DROP TABLE.

Non-technical users are exactly the people you must protect from this — they cannot read the SQL to check it. Databricks has no per-statement read-only role, so the guard is ours to build.

Every statement is classified before it runs

Read, write, or destructive — after stripping comments and string literals, so a keyword hidden inside a quoted value cannot smuggle itself past the classifier. Multi-statement, CREATE USER, LOAD DATA and INTO OUTFILE are refused outright.

Destructive statements never run silently

The SQL is shown in a Slack card, explained in plain English, and does not execute until a human clicks.

Malformed output is caught, not executed

A model that emits IN ('', '') returns "no rows" — which reads like a real answer and is far more dangerous than an error. Degenerate SQL is detected and regenerated.

Appends are verified against the target

A CSV whose columns don't match the table would write values into the wrong columns — a corruption you'd never notice. It's refused, with the mismatch named.

26 adversarial cases pass, including keyword-in-string-literal smuggling and stacked statements.
12
Product / Market Potential

Every company has data.
Most don't have a data team. That gap is the market.

This is not a cheaper BI tool

It is a BI tool with the technical prerequisite removed. We are not competing on price against Tableau or Power BI — we are addressing the customers who bounced off them entirely, because those products assume an operator the customer does not employ.

Beachhead: regulated, paper-heavy, no data team

Clinics, medical billing offices, dental and veterinary practices, small insurers, law firms, accountants. They have the most paper, the strictest data-handling rules, and the least in-house engineering. Nobody is serving them.

The moat is the deployment model

An incumbent built on a closed API cannot follow us into a hospital without re-architecting onto open weights. Our compliance story is a property of the architecture, not a certification we bought.

Unit economics

The cost of serving one more company is one more GPU-second — not one more data engineer. Open weights mean no per-token vendor tax, and inference cost falls with every generation of AMD silicon.

Distribution is already solved

Slack is the install base. There is no app to download, no new login, no training. The Slack App Directory is the channel.

Land and expand

Land on one painful workflow — "stop retyping the invoices". Expand into every question the team was already asking each other by hand. The lakehouse quietly accretes underneath.

13
Who Benefits

Everyone wins

For the non-technical user

  • ✦They stop waiting. The answer arrives in the window they already have open.
  • ✦They stop retyping. The scanned page becomes data.
  • ✦They can't break anything. Destructive changes need a click.
  • ✦They never learn the word "lakehouse".

For the business

  • ✦Decisions get made on data, not instinct, because asking is now free.
  • ✦Transcription error — a real clinical and billing risk — drops out of the workflow.
  • ✦Their data never leaves their infrastructure.
  • ✦Every question and change is audited in a channel, for free.

For AMD

  • ✦A commercial workload where open weights on owned silicon is a requirement, not a preference.
  • ✦Inference demand that scales with customers, not with engineers.
  • ✦An on-prem / private-cloud story that closed-API competitors structurally cannot match.
14
The Product

Data Wizard in Slack

Rich Block Kit surfaces — Data Table results, Cards with actions, and a modal that never lets a table be created without you naming it.

Drop a CSV → a Card with actions, and a real Data Table preview.
We never name a table after the file — it asks what you call it.
Existing table? Pick it from your live tables — then append or replace.
Loaded into the lakehouse — and Gemma wrote the SHOW TABLES too.
"Create a dashboard with hackathon_signups" → a real published Tableau workbook.
Everything you can say — no SQL anywhere.
15
Live Demo

What you'll see in the video

1 · The scanned invoice

A real scanned page — a picture, not data — goes into Slack. Gemma's vision on the AMD GPU reads the table off the image, and it lands in the lakehouse as typed rows.

2 · Name it like a human

The modal asks what to call it. Not q3_export_final_v2 — invoices.

3 · Ask it a question

Plain English. Gemma writes the SQL against the live schema, and the answer renders as a real Slack table.

4 · Build the lakehouse

"Build a medallion pipeline" → bronze, silver and gold, with lineage and dedup. They never touch a console.

5 · Publish a dashboard

"Create a dashboard with signups" → Gemma picks the chart; a real Tableau workbook is published and the chart posts back into Slack.

6 · Try to destroy something

"Drop the bronze table." The SQL is shown, explained, and waits for a human click.

16
Why This Should Win

The ones who need this most are the ones who can least afford to hand their data to anybody else.

Creativity

A scanned invoice becomes a governed table you can query in plain English — no console, no SQL, no BI licence.

Completeness

Ingest → model → govern → visualise → converse. Every stage is built, wired and verified end-to-end — not a demo path.

Use of AMD

Four capabilities on the AMD GPU, and a market that is only reachable because the weights are open and the hardware is yours.

Market potential

Millions of businesses with data, no data team, and rules that forbid the obvious shortcut.

We are solving a real problem — data handling made faster, simpler and accessible to everyone.

That gap is the market. And it is one sentence in Slack away from closing.

17
Reference

Everything a user can say

Every action a user can execute, each with a real message you can copy. No syntax to memorise — Gemma reads the live schema, so it answers against whatever tables actually exist.

📥 Load data

  • Upload a CSV — types inferred, preview card, then Load
    drag signups.csv into the DM
  • Upload a scanned PDF — Gemma's vision reads the table off the page
    drop invoice.pdf into the channel
  • Build a pipeline — bronze / silver / gold, one click
    click “Build medallion pipeline”

❓ Ask questions

  • Ask in plain English — Gemma writes the SQL, runs it, returns a table + the SQL
    @Data Wizard how many signups per country?
  • Rank & filter — any table, any phrasing
    @Data Wizard show me the top 5 countries in countries_gdp

🔍 Explore the lakehouse

  • Browse catalogs & tables — Gemma writes the SHOW itself
    @Data Wizard what catalogs are there?
  • Inspect a schema — columns and types on demand
    @Data Wizard what columns does signups_silver have?

🏗 Create objects

  • Create a schema — Gemma writes the DDL, you're moved into it
    @Data Wizard create a schema called sales
  • Create a catalog — same, one message
    @Data Wizard create a catalog called finance

🌐 Generate a table

  • Real data — web-sourced figures with citations
    @Data Wizard create a table of the top 10 countries by population
  • Synthetic data — fake rows for testing
    @Data Wizard generate 20 fake employees
  • Ambiguous? It asks: 🌐 real or 🎲 synthetic

📊 Dashboards · ⚠️ Changes

  • Publish a dashboard — a real Tableau workbook, rendered back into Slack
    @Data Wizard create a dashboard with hackathon_signups
  • Change or delete data — SQL is shown, explained, and waits for your “Run it” click
    @Data Wizard drop the bronze table
Also: @Data Wizard help · @Data Wizard context (where am I?) · @Data Wizard use catalog finance · @Data Wizard use schema sales
